skill.name: find-bugs
skill.description: Find bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues in local branch changes. Use when asked to review changes, find bugs, security review, or audit code on the current branch.
skill.summary: Review branch changes for bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality risks.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The external command finding is a true positive as a risk factor because the skill asks the agent to run a hardcoded git diff command. The two weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by prose words in Markdown, not cryptographic implementation. No prompt injection, exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Git Diff Command Guidance
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 12 instructs the agent to run `git diff master...HEAD`. This is a local read-only Git command with hardcoded arguments, so command injection risk is low, but it still requires external command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present in Markdown and matches the skill's review purpose. Risk is limited because no user-controlled shell interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md lines 3 and 66 as weak cryptography, but both are prose. Line 3 describes finding security vulnerabilities, and line 66 defines an output field named File:Line with a brief description.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain no cryptographic API, algorithm selection, secrets, or executable code. The static matches appear to be substring matches inside ordinary documentation text.

content.value_statement: Branch reviews often miss security issues when only style or syntax is checked. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a structured bug and security review.
actual_capabilities: Reviews local branch diffs against the master branch.
Maps changed code to inputs, database queries, auth checks, sessions, external calls, and crypto operations.
Applies a security checklist covering injection, XSS, CSRF, IDOR, sessions, DoS, and information disclosure.
Requires context checks before reporting each potential issue.
Formats findings with file location, severity, problem, evidence, fix, and references.
Reports clean results without inventing issues when no significant problems are found.
limitations: It depends on complete local diff access and surrounding file context.
It does not make code changes or apply fixes automatically.
It may miss issues outside the current branch changes.
It assumes `master` is the correct comparison branch.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Security Review
use_cases.description: Review a feature branch before opening or merging a pull request.

use_cases.title: Focused Vulnerability Triage
use_cases.description: Check recent code changes for practical security risks and missing tests.

use_cases.title: Release Quality Gate
use_cases.description: Run a structured bug and risk review before a release branch is finalized.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Branch Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of current branch changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the find-bugs skill to review my current branch for bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review changes with emphasis on exploitable risks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the find-bugs skill and prioritize security findings. Focus on injection, authentication, authorization, session handling, and information disclosure.

prompt_templates.title: High-Risk File Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review sensitive areas after a targeted change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the find-bugs skill on this branch. Pay extra attention to changed files that handle user input, database access, payments, or permissions.

prompt_templates.title: Release Gate Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before shipping a release branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the find-bugs skill as a release gate. Confirm every reviewed file, list unverified areas, and only report issues with concrete evidence and fixes.

output_examples.input: Review this branch before I open a pull request.
output_examples.output: The response lists reviewed files, then reports findings ordered by severity with file location, evidence, impact, and a concrete fix.

output_examples.input: Check whether this auth change has security problems.
output_examples.output: The response focuses on authentication, authorization, sessions, and missing tests, then states any areas that could not be verified.

output_examples.input: Find bugs in my payment workflow changes.
output_examples.output: The response maps inputs, database writes, external calls, and business logic before reporting only evidence-backed issues.

best_practices: Run the review from a clean working tree so the diff is clear.
Provide the intended base branch if it is not `master`.
Ask for verification notes when reviewing security-sensitive changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as the only review for large architectural changes.
Do not rely on it without reading the final evidence and fix suggestions.
Do not ask it to invent issues when no significant evidence exists.

faq.question: Does this skill change my code?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to report findings only, so the developer can decide what to fix.

faq.question: Which branch does it compare against?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the agent to compare the current branch against `master`.

faq.question: Can it review security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a checklist for common application security risks and asks for evidence before reporting.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the skill file.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Use it in a repository with the target branch checked out and the relevant changes available locally.
